name: Native HTTP Probes Demo
description: Showcases v1.18.0 native commands: each component's check / start / stop is described as a typed `*_native` HTTP probe instead of a shell command. The agent runs them via its built-in HTTP client (reqwest) — no curl, wget, Invoke-WebRequest needed on the host. Particularly useful on Windows where shipping curl.exe is awkward. Three components: a JSON-API frontend, a backing API, and a fictional 'admin' that exposes start/stop endpoints.

components.name: Backing API
components.// notes: When `check_native` is set, the agent ignores `check_cmd` and runs the typed probe instead. Same precedence applies to start_native and stop_native. exit 0 = healthy, exit 1 = unhealthy.
components.// auth_explanation: Use `bearer_token` for the common Authorization: Bearer pattern — the agent emits the header for you and the value is masked in logs/audit. For other schemes (Basic, mTLS, custom), set the full `Authorization` header in `headers` directly; that wins over `bearer_token`.

components.name: JSON Frontend
components.// fallback_explanation: No start_native / stop_native here — when those fields are absent, the agent falls back to start_cmd / stop_cmd if set. Both empty = the parent app's Start button is a no-op for this component (useful when something else manages it).

components.name: Postgres TCP
components.// tcp_probe: Native check doesn't have to be HTTP — `kind: tcp_connect` opens a TCP socket to host:port within timeout_seconds. Healthy when the socket opens. Useful for databases / queues / caches where you don't want to authenticate just to probe liveness.

notes.why_native: Lets a check / start / stop be described as a typed payload (HTTP probe, TCP connect, …) instead of a shell command. Avoids per-host quoting headaches and the recurring 'how do I install curl on Windows' problem. The shell `*_cmd` columns still work — when both shell and native are set on the same field, native wins.
notes.expect_status: Default = any 2xx is healthy. Set `expect_status: 200` to require the exact status, or skip it for permissive checks.
notes.expect_body_contains: Optional substring assertion. Useful for HTTP services that always return 200 with `{ status: "degraded" }` in the body.
notes.insecure_tls: Set `insecure: true` on an HTTP probe to skip TLS verification — for self-signed gear in non-production envs.
